mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening demand for goods and services that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.2 million signals an increase in supply of new homes that reduces housing prices and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month rise in industrial production signal a monetary easing cycle that boosts corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with declining federal funds rate and rising industrial production are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5% and a 2-month decline in industrial production signal a weakening labor market and demand for goods and services that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate and declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production and a 2-month rise in housing starts signal a weakening demand for goods and services and an increase in supply of new homes that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production and rising housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a monetary tightening cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
